document_purpose: Remaining development steps extracted from the merged plan (entity-scoped solution).

steps.title: Enum canon and drift gate
actions: Build canonical enum definitions with legacy aliases.
Validate drift across registry data and contracts.
Generate RFC-6902 normalization patches.
Apply normalization after backup.
validation: Expected: No enum drift detected - GATE PASSED (after normalization).

steps.title: Finish 7 stubbed analyzers
actions: Complete implementations for all stubbed analyzers used by Phase A and Phase C.
validation: Expected: Phase A complete: 21 columns populated per file.
Note: consolidated_runner.py is a separate whole-repo inventory tool.

steps.title: Fix column-name mismatches in 4 scripts
actions: text_normalizer: rename py_text_normalized_hash to py_canonical_text_hash.

component_extractor: rename py_classes_count to py_defs_classes_count, py_functions_count to py_defs_functions_count, py_methods_count to py_component_count, py_components_hash to py_defs_public_api_hash; update error return too.
complexity_visitor: rename py_cyclomatic_complexity to py_complexity_cyclomatic.
analyze_tests: add py_tests_executed (True) and py_pytest_exit_code; rename py_test_coverage_pct to py_coverage_percent.

steps.title: Add --json support to 11 scripts (10 standard plus component_id_generator)
actions: Add --json <output_file> support to each script (write result JSON to file or stdout).
Special case component_id_generator: keep positional args <file_id> <components_json> and add --json after those args.

steps.title: Fix capability_detector hash and quality_scorer breakdown
actions: capability_detector: compute py_capability_facts_hash as SHA-256 of sorted capability tags.
quality_scorer: complete generate_breakdown() for docstrings, lint, and complexity.

steps.title: Execute capability mapping step 3
actions: Generate FILE_PURPOSE_REGISTRY.json from repo analysis.

steps.title: Build file ID reconciliation layer
actions: Build sha256 to file_id and file_id to sha256 mappings.
Capture normalized enum values per file_id.
Validate file_id is 20-digit numeric and sha256 is 64-hex.
Ensure component IDs use file_id (not doc_id).

steps.title: Build transformation layer for Phase A columns
actions: Transform FILE_INVENTORY.jsonl facts into registry py_* columns.
Handle direct, rename, and partial mappings for 21 Phase A columns.

steps.title: Add run metadata collection
actions: Add py_analysis_run_id, py_analyzed_at_utc, py_analysis_success, py_toolchain_id, py_tool_versions, py_component_artifact_path via _collect_run_metadata().

steps.title: Generate and apply comprehensive patches
actions: Load capability mapping, transformed py_* columns, and reconciliation map.
Generate RFC-6902 patch set, back up registry, dry-run, then apply.

steps.title: Column runtime schema loader
actions: Load and merge governance sources.
Validate policies and export unified metadata.

steps.title: Dependency scheduler and trigger dispatcher
actions: Generate topological order of column dependencies.
Route derivations based on triggers.

steps.title: Derivation executor and core components
actions: Execute derivations and generate patches.
Resolve cross-registry lookups.
Write evidence with SHA-256 trail.

steps.title: Canonical entity set
actions: Declare files[] authoritative.
Move entities[] and entries[] to LEGACY_OVERLAYS.json.
Create REGISTRY_ENTITIES.json with v4 schema metadata.

steps.title: Entity schema v4
actions: Expand enums (repo_root_id includes GOV_REG_WORKSPACE, canonicality includes all 4 values).
Keep entity-only schema (no edge or generator fields).

steps.title: Resolve 51 inconsistencies
actions: Type A: reclassify 18 editorial columns as manual_patch_only.
Type B: add defaults and expand enums (status, entity_kind, canonicality).
Type C: document on-create immutable fields.
Type D: implement lookup formulas (asset_id, asset_family, asset_version, external_ref, external_system, etc.).

steps.title: Fix file_id resolver in pipeline
actions: Add _build_file_id_lookup() to build relative and absolute path to file_id mappings from registry JSON.
Use exact path match, then normalized match in analyze_file().
Add analyze_file.py to the Phase A script list.

steps.title: Implement Phase B in pipeline
actions: Replace Phase B no-op with script execution (60 second timeout).
Run quality_scorer last.
Add _build_script_args() for script-specific CLI patterns.
Build metrics JSON for quality_scorer and invoke with --metrics-json and --output.

steps.title: Implement Phase C in pipeline
actions: Stage 1: merge results into {"files": [...]}, write tmp/batch_registry.json, run similarity_clusterer and canonical_ranker.
Stage 2: read clusters, limit pairwise comparisons to multi-file groups.

Run structural_feature_extractor and extract_semantic_features per target vs peers; capture stdout, parse the JSON scores object from the All scores block, write tmp/<file_id>_structural.json and tmp/<file_id>_semantic.json.
Run file_comparator with score files and map py_overlap_best_match_file_path to py_overlap_best_match_file_id using file_id lookup.
Merge batch and pairwise results back into per-file dicts.

steps.title: File intake orchestrator (entity-only)
actions: Use runtime engine to process file events and write to REGISTRY_ENTITIES.json only.
Load capability registry from Week 1 outputs.

steps.title: Capability mapping migration and timestamp clustering
actions: Migrate Week 1 promotion to use runtime engine (no bundle-commit wrapper).
Generate .state/purpose_mapping/TIMESTAMP_CLUSTERS.json and link provenance where available.

steps.title: Simplified convergent report (5 entity signals)
actions: Implement 5-signal convergence scoring (fs_primitives, artifact_kind_inferred, py_analysis_complete, capability_tagged, temporal_clustered).
Apply bands: HIGH if score >= 5, MEDIUM if 3-4, LOW otherwise.

steps.title: End-to-end validation and documentation
validation: python -c "import json; r = json.load(open('01260207201000001250_REGISTRY/canonical_v4/REGISTRY_ENTITIES.json')); sample = r['files'][0]; py_cols = [k for k in sample.keys() if k.startswith('py_')]; print(f'py_* columns found: {len(py_cols)} of 37')"
actions: Verify file_id resolution (20-digit numeric) for analyzed files.
Verify hash determinism by running the same file twice.
Update documentation after validation passes.
